description: Detection Chokepoints — Sentinel Scheduled Analytics Rules. 13 rules covering the invariant prerequisites attackers can't bypass.

rules.displayName: LSASS Credential Dump — Non-Standard Process with Dump Mechanism
rules.description: Detects a non-standard process (outside System32 and Program Files) opening a handle to lsass.exe with credential-dump access rights where the CallTrace reveals MiniDumpWriteDump (dbgcore.dll, dbghelp.dll) or direct syscall evasion (UNKNOWN). Covers Mimikatz, nanodump, comsvcs.dll, ProcDump, HandleKatz, and 18+ additional variants. 24 tool variations tracked.
rules.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where FileName =~ "lsass.exe"
| where ActionType == "OpenProcess"
| extend GrantedAccess = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).GrantedAccess)
| extend CallTrace = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).CallTrace)
| where GrantedAccess in ("0x1010", "0x1FFFFF", "0x1410", "0x0810", "0x0040")
| where CallTrace has_any ("dbgcore.dll", "dbghelp.dll", "UNKNOWN")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in ("csrss.exe", "services.exe", "svchost.exe", "wininit.exe", "MsMpEng.exe", "sense.exe", "CSFalconService.exe", "SentinelAgent.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith "C:\\Windows\\System32" and InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith "C:\\Program Files"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, GrantedAccess, CallTrace, AccountName, DeviceId

rules.displayName: EDR Bypass — Non-Microsoft Kernel Driver Loaded
rules.description: Detects kernel driver loads where the driver is unsigned, revoked, or signed by a non-Microsoft publisher. Baseline rule for BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) landscape mapping. Covers EDRKillShifter, Terminator, AuKill, POORTRY/STONESTOP, and all known vulnerable drivers.
rules.query:
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where ActionType == "DriverLoaded"
| extend LoadedBy = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).LoadedImage)
| extend DriverFile = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).FileName)
| extend DriverHash = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).SHA256)
| extend SignedBy = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).SignedBy)
| extend SignatureStatus = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).SignatureStatus)
| where SignedBy !contains "Microsoft" or SignatureStatus != "Valid"
| where LoadedBy !in ("csrss.exe", "svchost.exe", "lsass.exe", "smss.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, DriverFile, DriverHash, SignedBy, SignatureStatus, LoadedBy

rules.displayName: EDR Bypass — Security Process Handle Request + Service Stop
rules.description: Detects processes opening handles to security agent executables (MsMpEng, CSFalconService, SentinelAgent) with PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS followed by service state changes. High-confidence EDR kill chain indicator.
rules.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where FileName =~ "lsass.exe" or FileName in ("MsMpEng.exe", "CSFalconService.exe", "SentinelAgent.exe", "SophosFileScanner.exe", "CylanceSvc.exe")
| where ActionType == "OpenProcess"
| extend GrantedAccess = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).GrantedAccess)
| where GrantedAccess == "0x1FFFFF"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, FileName, GrantedAccess
| sort by Timestamp desc

rules.displayName: Infostealer — Non-Browser Process Accessing Browser Credential Database
rules.description: Detects non-browser processes reading Chrome/Edge/Firefox credential database files (Login Data, Cookies, Local State, logins.json). Covers 12 infostealer families: LummaC2, Stealc, RedLine, Raccoon v2, Vidar 2.0, AMOS, EDDIESTEALER, Medusa, and Chrome App-Bound Encryption bypass variants.
rules.query:
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where FileName in ("Login Data", "Cookies", "Local State", "logins.json")
| where FolderPath has_any ("Chrome", "Edge", "Firefox", "Brave", "Opera")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in ("chrome.exe", "msedge.exe", "firefox.exe", "brave.exe", "opera.exe", "vivaldi.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, FileName, FolderPath, DeviceId

rules.displayName: Ransomware Pre-Encryption — Bulk Security Service Stop/Delete
rules.description: Detects sc.exe, net.exe, or taskkill.exe stopping or deleting security and backup services in rapid succession. Strong pre-encryption indicator observed in BlackBasta, Alphv/BlackCat, Akira, Qilin, and LockBit campaigns. This is the last detectable warning before file encryption begins.
rules.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where FileName in ("sc.exe", "net.exe", "net1.exe", "taskkill.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("stop ", "delete ", "/IM ")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Sophos", "WinDefend", "Sense", "WdNisSvc", "Veeam", "VSS", "wbengine", "MSSQL", "SQLWriter", "Acronis", "backup", "CrowdStrike", "SentinelAgent", "CSFalconService", "CylanceSvc")
| summarize StopCount = count(), DistinctServices = dcount(ProcessCommandLine), Commands = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 5) by DeviceName, bin(Timestamp, 2m)
| where StopCount >= 3 or DistinctServices >= 2
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, StopCount, DistinctServices, Commands

rules.displayName: Web Shell — Web Server Process Spawning OS Interpreter
rules.description: Detects IIS/Apache/Nginx web server processes (w3wp.exe, httpd, nginx, php-fpm) spawning command interpreters (cmd.exe, powershell.exe, bash, sh). The parent-child relationship is the web shell chokepoint — invariant across all 11 tracked shell families (China Chopper, Godzilla, Behinder, AntSword, LEMURLOOT, etc.).
rules.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "php-fpm.exe", "php-cgi.exe", "tomcat*.exe")
| where FileName in ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "bash", "sh", "python", "python3", "perl", "ruby")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, AccountName, DeviceId

rules.displayName: ClickFix — Browser/Explorer Spawning Interpreter with Encoded Command
rules.description: Detects scripting interpreters (powershell.exe, cmd.exe, mshta.exe) spawned by browsers, Explorer, or Windows Terminal with encoded or obfuscated command lines. Covers 9 ClickFix variants: ClickFix, FileFix, TerminalFix, DownloadFix, JackFix/GlitchFix, WebDAV ClickFix, InstallFix, Windows Terminal ClickFix, DNS-based ClickFix.
rules.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where FileName in ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "cmd.exe", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "rundll32.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in ("explorer.exe", "wt.exe", "WindowsTerminal.exe") or InitiatingProcessFileName endswith "chrome.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName endswith "msedge.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName endswith "firefox.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName endswith "brave.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName endswith "opera.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-enc ", "-EncodedCommand", "FromBase64String", "IEX", "Invoke-Expression", "DownloadString", "Invoke-WebRequest", "iwr ", "curl ", "nslookup ", "net use ", "mshta http", "mshta vbscript")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, DeviceId

rules.displayName: Renamed RMM Tool — Browser Download with Execution + Outbound Connection
rules.description: Detects browser-downloaded executables in user-writable paths that are subsequently executed. PE metadata mismatch (OriginalFilename != current filename) is the key signal. Covers AnyDesk, TeamViewer, ScreenConnect, UltraViewer, RustDesk, SimpleHelp, NetSupport, Atera — 9 tracked RMM families.
rules.query:
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".msi"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in ("chrome.exe", "msedge.exe", "firefox.exe", "brave.exe", "opera.exe")
| where FolderPath has_any ("Downloads", "Temp", "AppData")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, DeviceId

rules.displayName: Remote Execution — Service Installed from TEMP with Short Random Name
rules.description: Detects services installed from user-writable directories (TEMP, Downloads, AppData) with short random names (≤10 chars). Classic PsExec/Impacket/NetExec lateral movement signature. Service binary executes attacker commands via services.exe parent chain.
rules.query:
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where ActionType == "ServiceInstalled"
| extend ServiceName = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).ServiceName)
| extend ServiceFileName = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).FileName)
| where ServiceFileName has "\\Temp\\" or ServiceFileName has "\\tmp\\" or ServiceFileName has "\\Downloads\\" or ServiceFileName has "\\AppData\\" or ServiceFileName has "\\ProgramData\\"
| where strlen(ServiceName) <= 12
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ServiceName, ServiceFileName, DeviceId

rules.displayName: BYOSI — Non-Default Interpreter from Temp Path with Script Arguments
rules.description: Detects scripting interpreters (Python, PHP, Node.js, Ruby, Perl, etc.) executing from user-writable directories with script file arguments or inline execution flags. Covers 13+ interpreter languages and 8 known BYOSI variants including PolyDrop, IronNetInjector, and NodeLoader.
rules.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(5m)
| where FileName in ("python.exe", "python3.exe", "pythonw.exe", "php.exe", "php-cgi.exe", "node.exe", "ruby.exe", "perl.exe", "lua.exe", "AutoHotkey.exe", "AutoHotkey64.exe", "deno.exe", "bun.exe")
| where FolderPath has_any ("Temp", "tmp", "Downloads", "AppData", "Users\\Public", "Windows\\Temp")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".py", ".pyc", ".php", ".js", ".mjs", "-e ", "-c ", "--eval", "-exec", "-r ", "--require")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, DeviceId

rules.displayName: AiTM — Node.js User Agent on Entra ID Sign-In to M365 Apps
rules.description: Detects Entra ID sign-ins with Node.js HTTP client user agents (node, axios, undici, node-fetch) on high-value Microsoft 365 applications. Characteristic of Tycoon 2FA and EvilProxy AiTM kit relay tiers making server-to-server HTTP calls to relay victim sessions. Normal M365 sign-ins use browser user agents.
rules.query:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(5m)
| where ResultType == 0
| where UserAgent has_any ("node", "axios", "undici", "node-fetch")
| where AppDisplayName has_any ("OfficeHome", "Microsoft Authentication Broker", "Microsoft Graph", "Microsoft Exchange Online")
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName, UserAgent, IPAddress, ResultType, CorrelationId

rules.displayName: OAuth Device Code Phishing — MAB App Interactive Redemption
rules.description: Detects successful interactive OAuth device-code-flow sign-ins using the Microsoft Authentication Broker app ID (29d9ed98). Tycoon 2FA's device-code-grant variant hardcodes this client ID to obtain FOCI refresh tokens. End users in managed environments rarely initiate device-code flows to the Auth Broker interactively.
rules.query:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(5m)
| where AuthenticationProtocol == "deviceCode"
| where AppId == "29d9ed98-a469-4536-ade2-f981bc1d605e"
| where ResultType == 0
| where IsInteractive == true
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, AppId, AppDisplayName, AuthenticationProtocol, IsInteractive, IPAddress, CorrelationId

rules.displayName: Post-AiTM — Multi-Category Graph API Reconnaissance Burst
rules.description: Detects 4+ distinct Microsoft Graph API endpoint categories (role/cross-tenant/mailbox/contact/org) from a single UPN within 60 seconds. Human portal navigation touches 1-2 categories per session; 4+ in 60 seconds is mechanically impossible at human speed and indicates automated AiTM operator console recon. Correlate with preceding Node.js UA sign-in for full chain confirmation.
rules.query:
MicrosoftGraphActivityLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(5m)
| extend EndpointCategory = case(
    Resource has "roleManagement" or Resource has "directoryRole", "role-discovery",
    Resource has "tenantRelationships", "cross-tenant",
    Resource has "mailboxSettings" or Resource has "messages", "mailbox",
    Resource has "contactFolders" or Resource has "contacts", "contacts",
    Resource has "subscribedSkus" or Resource has "organization", "org-licensing",
    "other"
  )
| where EndpointCategory != "other"
| summarize EndpointCategories = dcount(EndpointCategory), TotalCalls = count(), DistinctEndpoints = dcount(Resource) by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where EndpointCategories >= 4
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, EndpointCategories, TotalCalls, DistinctEndpoints
